steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills the subject + verb slot before "68th in a global ranking." The subject is the constitution of Lithuania (singular), so the blank needs a singular subject pronoun plus a linking verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'their': possessive pronoun, plural. No linking verb, so the sentence has no main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'its': possessive pronoun, singular. Still no linking verb; "its 68th in a global ranking" is not a complete clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'it's': contraction of "it is." Plugging in: "it is 68th in a global ranking" — complete clause, singular subject + linking verb, grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice D — 'they're': contraction of "they are," plural. The constitution is one document, so plural disagrees with the antecedent. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
